validation_rules: require every resumable synthetic detached job to resolve through exactly one continuation packet before resume
reject missing continuation packets, packets missing required fields, and already consumed continuation packets
require heartbeat rows to stay liveness evidence only, never resume authority or provider/UI uptime evidence
require resource pressure to block dispatch as a recorded blocked decision rather than a silent pass
require resume success to stay resume-only and never claim work landed without closeout transition receipts
require worker skip receipts to dedupe without silently closing a claim

reject live bridge payloads, provider payload bodies, operator thread bodies, HUD/browser state, live phase runtime state, prompt shelf bodies, private memory bodies, website surface bodies, credentials, cookies, and release payloads
receipt_expectations: receipt must cite continuation packet, heartbeat, resource pressure, resume-once, duplicate-resume, closeout-transition, private-state scan, authority ceiling, anti-claim, and receipt path evidence
anti_claim: This standard governs public synthetic transport bridge-continuity fixtures and receipts only; it does not run live bridge transport, call providers, read operator HUD/browser/phase runtime state, prove provider or UI uptime, land work, mutate source, or authorize release.

anti_claim_contract.rule: Bridge-continuity language must state that synthetic heartbeat, resume, and closeout receipts are not live bridge health, provider/UI uptime, work-landing, source-mutation, or release authority.

source_coupling_contract.rule: Fixture refs, public source-module manifests, target refs, digests, validation refs, and body-in-receipt false receipts must stay coupled before the organ is widened.

omission_receipt.reason: public slice carries synthetic transport continuity rows, source refs, digests, body-free receipts, authority ceilings, and negative-case evidence only

standard_payload.ontology: A public synthetic transport continuity runtime that validates disk-first continuation packets, heartbeat/resource-pressure gating, resume-once semantics, worker-skip dedupe, closeout transition receipts, and private-state boundaries.
standard_payload.teleology: Make bridge and subagent continuity reusable by future agents without requiring them to rediscover the observe/apply fixture, continuation packet contract, receipt boundary, or live-authority ceiling.
standard_payload.anti_purpose: A bridge-runtime health claim, provider/UI uptime claim, or work-landing story inferred from synthetic heartbeat or continuation metadata.
failure_modes: resumable job accepted without a continuation packet
continuation packet missing required fields accepted
heartbeat freshness treated as resume authority
stale heartbeat treated as live runtime evidence
resource pressure block recorded as pass or omitted
resume receipt claims work landed without closeout transition receipt
worker skip receipt silently closes a claim
live bridge, provider, HUD/browser, live phase, prompt shelf, private memory, or website surface body exported
synthetic fixture receipt treated as provider/UI uptime, source-mutation, work-landing, or release authority
